summary: Tested Forge IDE agent-mode write flow with GLM 5.1 default. Model select correctly shows GLM 5.1, agent toggle shows orange 'Agent' badge, terminal prompt OK. However the core flow FAILED: after sending 'Create a file named hello.txt with the text Hi there', the assistant replied with a generic clarification message ('I'm ready to help! What would you like me to create or modify?') instead of emitting a <forge-file> block. GET /api/fs/read?path=hello.txt returned 404 — file was NOT created. Also, explorer does not show tree-example.py (shows example.py, test_t1.txt, upload_t1.txt, upreg.txt, welcome.md, t1folder_renamed/ only).
passed: Model select shows current value z-ai/glm-5.1 (GLM 5.1) — default applied
Agent mode badge visible (orange 'Agent' pill in header)
chat-input accepts text; chat-send-btn sends message

failed.issue: GLM 5.1 did NOT emit a <forge-file> block. It responded with a generic clarification: 'I’m ready to help! What would you like me to create or modify?' — same class of regression as iterations 1-4 (Llama refusing). The direct-curl verification mentioned in the task does NOT reproduce through the in-app chat path.
failed.evidence: Chat reply visible in screenshot; GET /api/fs/read?path=hello.txt -> 404 {"detail":"File not found"}; DELETE pre-step also returned 404 (file never existed). Prompt sent exactly: 'Create a file named hello.txt with the text Hi there'.

failed.issue: tree-example.py is NOT present in the explorer (0 occurrences in DOM). Explorer lists: t1folder_renamed/, example.py, test_t1.txt, upload_t1.txt, upreg.txt, welcome.md.
failed.evidence: Playwright count for text 'tree-example.py' = 0; screenshot of EXPLORER panel.

action_items: Investigate why GLM 5.1 in-app does NOT emit <forge-file> block while the direct curl test does. Likely the agent system prompt / message construction in /app/backend/routes/ai.py or the ChatPanel agent-reminder prefix is not being applied for the first-turn user message, or is being stripped. Compare the exact request body the frontend sends vs. the curl that worked.

Confirm whether the assistant's clarification reply ('I’m ready to help...') is a hard-coded greeting from the frontend or actually returned by the model — if frontend-injected, the user message may not be reaching the model. Inspect ChatPanel.jsx send path and backend stream handler.
Optionally: re-prompt with a stronger system message demanding the <forge-file> block, or fall back to a different model (Kimi K2.6 / MiniMax M3) and re-verify.
If tree-example.py was expected, seed the workspace with it; otherwise update the test expectation.
